title: Empirical Evaluation of Secured Intra-Data Communication via Human Body using Wearable Technology Association
exclusion_reason: The primary focus of the paper is on secure data transmission using Body Coupled Communication (BCC) and a general IoT health monitoring framework. While it mentions the use of biometrics as part of a multi-factor authentication (MFA) system to access the network, it does not propose, evaluate, or analyze a biometric authentication/identification system itself. The reported metrics focus on transmission rates, latency, and anomaly detection for health monitoring rather than biometric performance (e.g., EER, FAR).
